_doc: Per-op parity state. Updated by `parity-sweep sweep` runs and the orchestrator. status ∈ {missing, stub, diverges, verified, verified_with_deferred}. known_divergences are preserved as cheap regression seeds — never delete them, even when the bug is fixed. fixed_divergences holds entries that were resolved and migrated out of known_divergences; both lists are persistent.

add.pytorch_source: aten/src/ATen/native/BinaryOps.cpp (add_stub) + torch/overrides.py (signature: input, other, *, alpha=1, out=None)

fixed_divergences.root_cause: ferrotorch::grad_fns::arithmetic::add signature was (a, b) — had no alpha parameter. torch computes a + alpha*b.

fast_add / fast_mul / fast_sub / fast_div / scalar_map (ferrotorch-core/src/ops/elementwise.rs) called Tensor::data() which Errs on non-contiguous views. torch.add handles transposed/permuted views transparently. The same-shape SIMD fast paths now materialize non-contig inputs via .contiguous() before reading data(), matching binary_map's existing handling.
fixed_divergences.fix_ref: ferrotorch-core/src/ops/elementwise.rs fast_add / fast_mul / fast_sub / fast_div / scalar_map (re-corrector)

fixed_divergences.root_cause: ferrotorch::Tensor::add_ had no alpha kwarg and rejected broadcasting (torch's add_(other, *, alpha=1) supports both as long as the broadcast result equals self.shape()). Added add_scaled_(&self, other, alpha) at ferrotorch-core/src/inplace.rs:160 that routes through grad_fns::arithmetic::add_scaled and swaps storage in-place; add_ is now a thin alpha=1.0 alias.

ferrotorch had no `out=` kwarg / pre-allocated-output API. torch.add(*, out=t) writes into the provided tensor (and silently resizes it if its shape differs from the broadcast result — with a UserWarning that flags this as deprecated in future PyTorch). Implemented inline as `grad_fns::arithmetic::add_out` (alpha=1.0 alias) and `add_scaled_out(&out, &a, &b, alpha)` which (a) validates device match, (b) computes the result via the existing `add_scaled` path under `no_grad` (since `out=` is non-autograd-tracked per torch), (c) swaps the result storage into `out` via `update_storage` when shapes match or `update_storage_and_shape` when `out` must be resized. Required adding `Tensor::update_storage_and_shape` (ferrotorch-core/src/tensor.rs) which mirrors `update_storage`'s Arc<TensorInner> cast pattern and also rewrites shape/strides/offset in lockstep. Runner wires the `out_spec` kwarg through `materialize_ferrotorch_tensor` so the `out` envelope's fill (including NaN) is preserved before the write, then returns the post-write tensor's contents.
fixed_divergences.fix_ref: ferrotorch-core/src/grad_fns/arithmetic.rs add_out / add_scaled_out + ferrotorch-core/src/tensor.rs update_storage_and_shape (re-corrector). Originally tracked as #1190; the cross-cutting concern dissolved when the first `_out` variant landed — `_out` as a function-naming convention is fine for now, and a workspace-wide trait can settle later when more ops grow `_out` variants. Issue #1190 closed.

discriminator_strong_passes: NaN propagation (7/7): finite + NaN, NaN at any position, alpha=0 * NaN, alpha=NaN — all NaN positions match torch exactly.
+/-Inf and overflow (8/8): inf+inf, inf-inf=NaN, max+max=inf via F32_MAX token, inf*alpha=inf, 0*inf=NaN — all match.
Denormals (6/6): subnormal + 0, subnormal + subnormal, alpha-scaled subnormals — preserved, no flush-to-zero.
Empty tensors (6/6): [0], [0,5], [5,0], [2,0,3], empty + non-empty broadcast, alpha != 1 — all shape-preserving.
Scalar 0-dim tensors (6/6): [] preserved, [] + [3,4] broadcasts to [3,4], 0-dim NaN/Inf handled.
0-stride broadcasting (6/6): expand([1] -> [5,5]) + [5,5], expand row, NaN through expand — torch parity holds.
Alpha edges (9/9): alpha=0, alpha=-0.0, alpha=1.0 (fast path), alpha=2 (int) vs alpha=2.0 (float) bit-exact, alpha=tiny/huge/neg-huge, alpha=-1 (sub-equivalent).
Autograd identity (6/6): da=grad and db=alpha*grad correct on broadcast, alpha=2, alpha=-0.5, alpha=0; only-a requires_grad correctly produces grad_b=None.

Non-contig stride (6/6 after re-corrector): transpose_2d, transpose_both, transpose_3d, transpose_with_alpha, transpose_with_nan, transpose_already_in_strong_passes all materialize via .contiguous() and produce torch-bit-equivalent output.
In-place (5/5 after re-corrector): inplace_alpha + inplace_broadcast now route through add_scaled_; existing inplace probes unaffected.

out= kwarg (5/5 after re-corrector): out_basic, out_with_alpha, out_broadcast, out_wrong_shape (silently resizes `out` to broadcast shape, matching torch's current 2.x behavior + deprecation warning), out_nan (pre-fill NaN fully overwritten — no leak). Implemented via add_scaled_out + Tensor::update_storage_and_shape.

fixed_divergences.root_cause: op_db's {beta:0, alpha:0} degenerate sample has self=[NaN]. Upstream addr_kernel (aten/src/ATen/native/cpu/LinearAlgebraKernel.cpp:53-55) DROPS the self term when beta==0 so NaN/inf in self do not propagate (torch out=0). ferrotorch's addr forward used to compute beta*self literally (0.0*NaN=NaN), so the runner legitimate-skipped this exact degenerate (8/48). FIXED in grad_fns/linalg.rs commit 1a43ddeee (#1598): addr_differentiable now branches on beta==0 and computes alpha*outer(vec1,vec2) only (the self/bias buffer is NEVER read), matching torch. The runner skip guard was removed 2026-05-28 (#1344 addr-skip cleanup); addr now reaches 48/48 passed (0 skipped, 0 failed).
fixed_divergences.fix_ref: ferrotorch-core/src/grad_fns/linalg.rs addr_differentiable beta==0 branch (commit 1a43ddeee, #1598); runner skip removed in tools/parity-sweep/runner/src/main.rs "addr" arm (#1344)

known_divergences.root_cause: op_db emits 3-D [5,5,5] samples with non-default dim1/dim2 (3/15 per seed). ferrotorch's diagonal forward is the 2-D dim1=0,dim2=1 contract (REQ-30); runner legitimate-skips non-2-D inputs (24/120). Batched diagonal is a residual under #1344.

ferrotorch ops/cumulative.rs:49-56 validate_dim rejects ndim==0. Upstream torch.cumsum(torch.tensor(5.0), 0) returns tensor(5.) per ReduceOps.cpp:503-504 scalar branch.
Only lib.rs:159 re-export; no ferrotorch-*/src/ caller outside tests. Runner dispatch arm is a TEST-SIDE consumer per R-DEFER-1.
Only lib.rs:159 re-export; runner dispatch is TEST-SIDE per R-DEFER-1.

ferrotorch's CumprodBackward zeros-path at grad_fns/cumulative.rs:142-160 is O(n^3) brute-force, not upstream's masked-fill composite-compliance algorithm. Numerically correct (test_cumprod_backward_with_zero verifies 1/8/0 expected gradients) but slower and not second-order-differentiable. Not a forward parity issue; not blocking the parity-sweep gate.

known_divergences.root_cause: cummax_forward errors `cannot operate on a scalar (0-D) tensor`. Upstream returns (tensor(5.), tensor(0)) for a 0-D input.
ferrotorch ops/cumulative.rs:247 uses strict `>` so EARLIER index wins on equal values; upstream uses std::greater_equal so LATER wins.
grad_fns/cumulative.rs:230 has no grad-fn; upstream derivatives.yaml:533-535 specifies cummaxmin_backward (scatter_add via indices).
Upstream ReduceOps.cpp:819 isnan_(curr_elem) propagates NaN through running max; ferrotorch's strict `>` after NaN comparison keeps prior non-NaN value.

Production fix needed in ferrotorch-nn/src/conv.rs (OUTSIDE the #1441 test-infra manifest); contradicts the REQ-6/#1608 'dilated forward verified' claim for the 3-D rank.

nn.functional.dropout.runner_arm: tools/parity-sweep/runner/src/main.rs "nn.functional.dropout" arm: !training and p==0 -> production dropout identity; p==1+training -> zeros_like (exact torch all-zeros, RNG-independent); p==1+eval -> identity; 0<p<1+training -> Ok(None) (#1441)
nn.functional.dropout.tolerance: default rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-7 (identity / exact zeros, exact)
known_divergences: 0 < p < 1 + training -> Ok(None) skip: Bernoulli mask drawn from ferrotorch's own RNG is not byte-comparable to torch's Philox mask (genuinely unrepresentable for byte-parity, not a failure). 4 stochastic configs/seed x 8 seeds = 32 skips. dropout2d/dropout3d arms absent (#1441).

nn.functional.dropout2d.pytorch_source: torch/nn/functional.py nn.functional.dropout2d(input, p=0.5, training=True, inplace=False) (per-channel feature dropout via _VF.feature_dropout); aten/src/ATen/native/Dropout.cpp:64 if (p == 0 || !train || numel == 0) return input; Dropout.cpp:68 if (p == 1) return multiply(input, at::zeros({}, input.options())); Dropout.cpp:73-74 make_feature_noise(input).bernoulli_(1 - p) (one Bernoulli per [N,C] channel)
nn.functional.dropout2d.ferrotorch_source: pub struct Dropout2d + <Dropout2d as Module>::forward in ferrotorch-nn/src/dropout.rs (per-channel MT19937 mask via ferrotorch_core::rng::with_thread_rng, ctor rejects p>=1, eval/p==0 identity short-circuit); non-test consumer: pub use dropout::Dropout2d in ferrotorch-nn/src/lib.rs
nn.functional.dropout2d.runner_arm: tools/parity-sweep/runner/src/main.rs "nn.functional.dropout2d | nn.functional.dropout3d" arm: !training or p==0 -> production Dropout2d::new + Module::eval + Module::forward identity short-circuit; p==1+training -> zeros_like (exact torch all-zeros, RNG-independent); p==1+eval -> identity; 0<p<1+training -> Ok(None) skip (#1441 runner-arm half)
nn.functional.dropout2d.tolerance: default rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-7 (identity / exact zeros, exact)
known_divergences: 0 < p < 1 + training -> Ok(None) skip: per-channel Bernoulli mask drawn from ferrotorch's own byte-exact MT19937 Generator is NOT byte-comparable to torch's mask without torch-seed-sync, which the sweep architecture lacks (oracle's sample() calls torch.manual_seed(seed) then sample_inputs(), consuming an opaque non-reproducible number of RNG draws BEFORE F.dropout2d draws its channel mask; the sweep loop passes no seed to dispatch_f32 and the dispatch arm never receives torch's expected tensor, so neither seed-sync nor an in-arm per-channel invariant check is possible). Genuinely unrepresentable for byte-parity, not a failure. 3 stochastic configs/seed x 8 seeds = 24 skips. RNG-plumbing gap tracked under #1452.

nn.functional.dropout3d.tolerance: default rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-7 (identity / exact zeros, exact)
known_divergences: 0 < p < 1 + training -> Ok(None) skip: per-channel Bernoulli mask drawn from ferrotorch's own byte-exact MT19937 Generator is NOT byte-comparable to torch's mask without torch-seed-sync, which the sweep architecture lacks (oracle's sample() calls torch.manual_seed(seed) then sample_inputs(), consuming an opaque non-reproducible number of RNG draws BEFORE F.dropout3d draws its channel mask; the sweep loop passes no seed to dispatch_f32 and the dispatch arm never receives torch's expected tensor, so neither seed-sync nor an in-arm per-channel invariant check is possible).
